blog_posts.title: Hazardous Scrap Metal Items That Need Special Handling
blog_posts.excerpt: Not all metal can go straight onto the scale. Learn which hazardous and restricted metal items need special handling or cannot be accepted at our yards.
blog_posts.content:
Some metal items are considered hazardous or restricted and require special handling, separate drop-off, or may not be accepted at all. This guide explains the most common hazardous scrap items we see and what to do with them.

### Why certain metal items are hazardous
Metals can become hazardous because of chemicals, oils, lead, mercury, PCBs, or pressure inside containers. These hazards can put employees, customers, and the environment at risk.

### Common hazardous or restricted metal items
Below are examples of hazardous or restricted metal items. Always contact us before bringing any of these:

1. **Lead-acid batteries** – These contain lead and corrosive acid and cannot be thrown into regular scrap piles. They must stay upright and unbroken in approved containers.
2. **Mercury-containing switches** – Often found in older vehicles and industrial equipment. Mercury is highly toxic and must be removed and processed separately.
3. **PCB ballasts** – Some older fluorescent light ballasts contain polychlorinated biphenyls (PCBs), which are regulated hazardous waste.
4. **Radioactive scrap (including contaminated metals)** – Any item marked with a radiation symbol or that has been used in medical or industrial radiation applications requires special approval and cannot be accepted at regular yards.
5. **Pressurized gas cylinders** – Propane tanks, oxygen cylinders, and similar containers can explode if cut or crushed and must be depressurized and certified empty.

### What we can do for you
In many cases, we can accept these items through a special program or partner facility. Procedures vary by location and material.

### How to check before you arrive
If you’re not sure whether your item is hazardous, contact us with details such as:
- Item type and approximate age
- Any markings, labels, or hazard symbols
- Photos of the item and any nameplates

Our team will advise whether we can take it, what documentation is required, and which yard or partner facility can help.

> Always call ahead before bringing hazardous or restricted items such as lead-acid batteries, mercury-containing switches, PCB ballasts, radioactive scrap, or pressurized gas cylinders.

blog_posts.title: How Residential Scrap Metal Pickup Works
blog_posts.excerpt: Step-by-step guide to scheduling a home pickup for appliances and other household metal items.
blog_posts.content:
Our residential pickup service makes it easy to recycle bulky metal items like refrigerators, washers, dryers, exercise equipment, and more.

### Step 1: Check your ZIP code
Use the Residential Pickup page to enter your ZIP code and confirm availability.

### Step 2: Choose your items
Select the item type (for example, Appliance → Refrigerator) and the quantity. Be as accurate as possible so we can send the right truck.

### Step 3: Pick a date and time window
Available days and time windows will appear based on your ZIP code. Morning windows typically end before 12:00 PM, while afternoon windows run later.

### Step 4: Prepare your items
Unplug appliances, remove food, and ensure a clear path to the curb or driveway. For refrigerators, doors must be secured or removed.

### Step 5: Get paid
In most areas, you’ll receive payment after the material is weighed at the yard. Options can include cash, check, or digital payout where available.

blog_posts.title: Recycling Programs for Small Manufacturing Shops
blog_posts.excerpt: Compare plan options designed for machine shops and small manufacturers, including scheduled monthly pickups.
blog_posts.content:
Small and mid-sized manufacturers generate valuable metal scrap, but managing it shouldn’t be a distraction from production.

We offer several business recycling plans tailored to manufacturing, including options with:
- Monthly scheduled pickup routes
- On-demand roll-off or gaylord swaps
- Container placement at no or low cost

Our Starter Manufacturing plan is specifically designed for shops with light to moderate scrap volume and a budget under $200 per month.

blog_posts.title: Non-Ferrous Metals: Copper, Aluminum, and More
blog_posts.excerpt: Overview of our most common non-ferrous metals, including high-value copper grades.
blog_posts.content:
Non-ferrous metals like copper and aluminum are among the most valuable materials we buy.

### Common copper items
Examples include:
- Bare bright copper wire
- #1 insulated copper wire
- #2 insulated copper wire
- Copper pipe and tubing

Prices vary by yard and market conditions, and many clean copper items pay more than $2.00 per pound.

blog_posts.title: Choosing the Right Scrap Metal Dumpster Size
blog_posts.excerpt: A quick comparison of 10-yard, 20-yard, and larger metal dumpsters for projects and cleanouts.
blog_posts.content:
We offer 10-yard, 20-yard, 30-yard, and 40-yard scrap metal dumpsters in select ZIP codes. For many small projects, a 20-yard box offers the best balance between capacity and placement space.

Use the Container Rental page to enter your ZIP code, compare daily rates, and select a rental period that fits your schedule.

blog_posts.title: Understanding Scrap Metal Pricing and Payout Options
blog_posts.excerpt: Learn how prices are set, how to compare pickup vs. drop-off, and which payment methods are available.
blog_posts.content:
Our Pricing & Quotes page lets you compare on-site pickup vs. drop-off at a yard for many material types. In general, drop-off at a yard pays a higher rate per pound because there are fewer logistics costs.

You can also see which payout methods are available in your area, such as cash, check, or PayPal where supported.

blog_posts.title: Plan Your Drop-off Visit Like a Pro
blog_posts.excerpt: Use our Plan Drop-off tool to estimate weights, choose materials, and speed up your yard visit.
blog_posts.content:
The Plan Drop-off tool lets you select materials from our Materials Guide and assign estimated weights. When you arrive at the scale, our team can quickly match your plan and get you processed faster.

Pro tip: Separate high-value non-ferrous items, like copper and aluminum, from mixed steel to maximize your payout.

blog_posts.title: First-Time Customer Drop-off Promotions Explained
blog_posts.excerpt: Details on how new customers can use promo codes when booking a drop-off appointment.
blog_posts.content:
We periodically offer first-time customer promotions for drop-off visits at select yards. To redeem, book a drop-off appointment online, choose your preferred yard (such as our Main Yard), and enter the current first-time customer promo code on the booking form.

The discount is typically applied to fees or boosts your payout rate on qualifying materials.

blog_posts.title: Aluminum Cans and Siding: Recycling Basics
blog_posts.excerpt: Everything you need to know about recycling aluminum cans and siding for maximum payout.
blog_posts.content:
Aluminum is one of the most commonly recycled non-ferrous metals. Clean aluminum cans and siding often pay some of the highest prices per pound among household items.

Check the Locations & Yards page to compare aluminum can pricing by yard and find the highest-paying drop-off location near you.

blog_posts.title: Updated Policy on Restricted and Special-Handling Items
blog_posts.excerpt: Summary of our latest policy for restricted items that require documentation or advance approval.
blog_posts.content:
Certain items, such as catalytic converters, utility-owned materials, and traffic signs, require proof of ownership or other documentation before we can purchase them. This policy is in place to prevent theft and comply with local laws.

If your material might be restricted, contact us ahead of time with details so we can confirm what paperwork you’ll need.

business_plans.name: Manufacturing Starter Plan
business_plans.description: Designed for small manufacturing shops and machine shops generating light to moderate scrap volumes. Includes one monthly scheduled pickup, basic container placement, and online reporting.

business_plans.name: Manufacturing Growth Plus
business_plans.description: For growing manufacturers with higher scrap volumes. Includes up to two scheduled pickups per month, priority on-call service, and detailed rebate tracking.

business_plans.name: Construction Standard Plan
business_plans.description: Container-based program for contractors, with flexible short-term dumpster rentals and on-demand swaps.

business_plans.name: Retail Recycling Lite
business_plans.description: Entry-level program for retail stores with occasional metal fixtures and equipment to recycle.

business_plans.name: Healthcare Quarterly Bundle
business_plans.description: Quarterly pickup program for healthcare facilities with mixed metal equipment and fixtures.

materials.name: Aluminum Cans
materials.description: Loose or bagged aluminum beverage cans that are empty and mostly free of liquid and debris.

materials.name: Aluminum Siding
materials.description: Clean aluminum siding free of excessive insulation, tar, or non-metal attachments.

materials.name: Copper Wire
materials.description: General copper wire with varying insulation thickness. Pricing may adjust based on cleanliness and copper recovery.

materials.name: Bare Bright Copper Wire
materials.description: Clean, uncoated, unalloyed copper wire that is bright and shiny with no insulation or attachments.

materials.name: #1 Insulated Copper Wire
materials.description: High-grade insulated copper wire with a high copper recovery percentage and minimal contamination.

materials.name: #2 Insulated Copper Wire
materials.description: Lower-grade insulated copper wire with more insulation or mixed conductor types.

materials.name: Copper Pipe & Tubing
example_items: AC line sets (copper removed from insulation)
materials.description: Copper pipe and tubing that is free of excessive solder, fittings, and non-metal attachments.

materials.name: Steel Beams
materials.description: Heavy structural steel sections from construction, demolition, or fabrication projects.

materials.name: Mixed Steel & Shred
materials.description: Mixed ferrous scrap including light gauge steel and shred-ready material.

materials.name: Stainless Steel (Non-magnetic)
materials.description: Higher-value stainless steel grades that are mostly non-magnetic and corrosion-resistant.

materials.name: Refrigerator (Appliance)
materials.description: Standard residential refrigerators and freezers. Must be empty of food; doors should be secured or removed for pickup.

materials.name: Washing Machine / Dryer
materials.description: Household washers and dryers. Please disconnect water and power and ensure units are drained.

materials.name: Lead-Acid Batteries
materials.description: Lead-acid batteries containing lead and corrosive electrolyte. Must be upright, unbroken, and never placed in regular scrap piles.

materials.name: Mercury-Containing Switches
materials.description: Switches and relays that contain elemental mercury. These require special handling and may only be accepted at designated facilities.

materials.name: PCB Ballasts (Fluorescent Light)
example_items: Legacy fluorescent light ballasts with PCB markings
materials.description: Older fluorescent light ballasts that may contain PCBs. Subject to strict regulations and only processed through approved channels.

promotions.description: Save on residential scrap metal pickups this spring. Applies to qualifying appliance and metal pickups in select ZIP codes.

promotions.name: Contractor Dumpster Discount
promotions.description: Ongoing contractor program: get a daily rate discount on qualifying 10-yard and 20-yard scrap metal dumpsters.

promotions.name: Manufacturing Plan Intro Offer
promotions.description: Introductory offer on Manufacturing Starter business recycling plans for small shops signing a 12-month agreement.

promotions.name: New Customer Drop-off Special 2025 (Expired)
